checkpoints.trigger: before implementing photo handling in capture.py / common.py
checkpoints.question: Telegram photos land as files inside the sandboxed chat container, whose filesystem is ephemeral — but eBay needs the images days later at publish time, and the dashboard needs them for review. Options: (a) capture.py uploads every photo to the private Supabase Storage bucket at capture time (durable immediately, costs upload on every capture including never-approved drafts); (b) store container paths only and have a host-side worker re-fetch from Telegram by file_id later (no sandbox upload, but Telegram file_ids expire and the host worker needs bot-token access + more moving parts); (c) hybrid — capture uploads thumbnails only, full-res re-fetched at approve time. Which persistence model, considering the sandbox's stdlib-only constraint and that a failed upload must not lose the listing?
checkpoints.on_synthesis: implement exactly the chosen persistence model in capture.py/common.py, including its failure mode (photo upload failure must degrade to a draft row with a noted gap, never a lost listing)

checkpoints.trigger: before finalizing the sandbox capture skill's scope
checkpoints.question: The sandboxed capture skill can reach only Supabase REST. How much should it do itself? Options: (a) full capture — uploads photos, writes the complete draft row (title/description/appraisal), one-shot, no host involvement until pricing; (b) thin queue — write a minimal capture-request row (like the spec-intake bridge) and let a host cron enrich/normalize it into a draft (more robust validation and secrets stay host-side, but adds a worker + latency and the sandbox already holds the Supabase service key anyway); (c) full capture plus a host-side validation pass that quarantines malformed rows. Which boundary, given the service key is already forwarded to the sandbox and the row is reviewed by a human before anything irreversible?
checkpoints.on_synthesis: implement exactly the chosen boundary across capture.py and (if chosen) the host-side worker, and reflect it in SKILL.md and README.md
